finding_results.evidence: chapters/chapter_18.md:13, chapters/chapter_18.md:19, chapters/chapter_18.md:73, chapters/chapter_18.md:75, chapters/chapter_18.md:79, chapters/chapter_18.md:89
finding_results.notes: Full constitution audit performed. Primary craft issue identified: recurring 'the way [X] [verb]' analogical formula appeared ~12 times in the chapter, well above the constitution's threshold of 2 per chapter (rule 9). Seven edits applied across six lines to reduce to 6 instances, retaining only the strongest uses (lines 3, 13, 15, 79, 81). Secondary issue: 'Something about' triple anaphora in the not-reporting paragraph (line 19) broken by reworking third instance to 'And then Simone herself' — preserves rhetorical escalation without formulaic repetition. All other constitution checks passed on audit: heading contract preserved (line 1), no elliptical clipping, no over-formal dialogue (all dialogue uses contractions per character contraction_level: high), no sanitization (emotional aftermath rendered with full specificity — financial amounts named, Nora's not-reporting rationale explored without euphemism, Simone's guilt grounded in specific behaviors rather than generic remorse), no procedural drift displacing embodied consequence, all must-land beats present, continuity verified against continuity sheet ($12,900 total, debt over $30k consistent with sheet totals minus payments, timeline April-July consistent with seasonal track, Simone's move to Philadelphia and OpenClaw deletion consistent with state transitions). Character voices verified: Nora's editorial vocabulary and warm-specific noticing maintained throughout sections 1 and 3; Simone's transactional interiority, financial tallying, and tech vocabulary maintained in section 2; Margo's directness and Diane's maternal-fear-through-food both rendered per style bible profiles. No style bible example lines appear in the chapter.

summary: Chapter 18 audited against constitution, style bible, and continuity sheet. One craft defect found and fixed: recurring prose formula ('the way [X] [verb]' analogical construction) appeared ~12 times, constituting a tic per constitution rule 9. Reduced to 6 instances via 7 targeted edits that vary syntactic construction while preserving meaning and voice. Secondary fix: broke triple 'Something about' anaphora in not-reporting paragraph. All edits preserve the chapter's quiet-ambiguous-final texture, character voice signatures, and continuity accuracy. No sanitization introduced — the chapter's full emotional specificity maintained throughout.
